summary: Matched two more functions in sysdolphin/baselib gobjgxlink by replacing asm for func_8039075C and func_803907C8 with C implementations named GObj_SetupGXLinkMax and GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted. The PR also propagated those names through many remaining asm callsites and added prototypes to gobjgxlink.h, making GX render-link setup behavior more searchable and reusable.
what_changed: Deleted the asm bodies for func_8039075C and func_803907C8 from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s, indicating those functions were matched in C.

Added GObj_SetupGXLinkMax in src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c. It sets render_cb, assigns gx_link to lbl_804CE380.gx_link_max + 1, stores render_priority, walks lbl_804D7820[gobj->gx_link] using prev_gx while existing priority is greater, then calls GObj_GXReorder.
Added an inline helper GObj_GXFindPrioPosition that starts from lbl_804D7824[gobj->gx_link] and walks next_gx while existing render_priority is less than the new object's priority.

Added GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted in C. It shares the max-link setup logic, uses GObj_GXFindPrioPosition, adjusts to prev_gx when a position is found or falls back to lbl_804D7820[gobj->gx_link], then reorders the object.
Added public prototypes for GObj_SetupGXLinkMax and GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted to src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.h.
Updated include/functions.h comments from unknown func_8039075C and func_803907C8 entries to GObj_SetupGXLinkMax and GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted.
Renamed many remaining assembly callsites from func_8039075C to GObj_SetupGXLinkMax across camera, game-mode, ground, interface, menu, text, sislib, and sobjlib asm files.
Renamed at least one callsite from func_803907C8 to GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted, shown in asm/melee/gr/grizumi.s.
smart_moves: The names were based on observed behavior rather than only addresses: both functions force the GObj onto gx_link_max + 1, so the "Max" suffix captures the distinctive setup semantics.

The PR separated the sorted variant from the non-sorted/max variant. GObj_SetupGXLinkMax walks from lbl_804D7820 with a greater-than priority test, while GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted uses a helper that walks from lbl_804D7824 with a less-than priority test before converting the found node to a reorder anchor.
Adding gobjgxlink.h prototypes immediately made the matched functions available to future C decomp work instead of leaving them as local-only knowledge.

Renaming all known asm callsites in the same PR greatly improved cross-module grepability and helped document function arity and argument roles through repeated usage patterns: gobj, render callback, priority.
Using a small inline helper for the sorted search likely helped reproduce the compiler output for func_803907C8 while keeping the source readable.
decomp_lessons: When a function is matched, propagate the new symbol name through still-asm callsites; this preserves project-wide context and makes future decompilation easier.

For baselib GObj functions, repeated callsite signatures are strong evidence for parameter naming: r3 is the HSD_GObj pointer, r4 is the render callback, and r5 is the render priority in these setup routines.
GX-link list manipulation can require reasoning from both list heads/tails: lbl_804D7820 and lbl_804D7824 are used differently by the two matched functions.

Address-based unknowns like func_8039075C and func_803907C8 can often be named by comparing nearly identical code paths and identifying the semantic difference, here max-link setup versus max-link sorted setup.
Inline helper extraction can be a valid matching tactic when it mirrors a reusable search subexpression and does not introduce a separate exported symbol.
naming_conventions: Use the existing baselib GObj prefix style for matched functions: GObj_SetupGXLink, GObj_SetupGXLinkMax, GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted, GObj_GXReorder.
Use descriptive suffixes to distinguish close variants: "Max" indicates assignment to gx_link_max + 1, while "Sorted" indicates the alternate priority-position search path.
Use HSD_GObj* and render_cb parameter naming consistently for GObj render-link setup routines.
The internal helper was named GObj_GXFindPrioPosition, using the GObj_GX prefix and a short "Prio" abbreviation consistent with render-priority semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched func_8039075C by expressing stores to gobj fields as gobj->render_cb, gobj->gx_link, and gobj->render_priority, then reproducing the loop over prev_gx until i is NULL or i->render_priority is not greater than the new priority.
Matched func_803907C8 by expressing the tail-list search over next_gx, then converting the found node into the correct high-priority reorder anchor using prev_gx or lbl_804D7820[gobj->gx_link].
Preserved the max-link byte behavior by using u8 max_link = lbl_804CE380.gx_link_max followed by gobj->gx_link = max_link + 1.
The deleted asm confirms the C matched the original machine code for both 8039075C and 803907C8; all callsite instruction addresses and branch targets remained unchanged except symbol labels.
The broad asm rename pass turned raw branches to func_8039075C/func_803907C8 into semantic calls without changing instruction bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert remaining GObj_SetupGXLinkMax callsites from asm to C.
Search for subsequent naming or struct changes around lbl_804CE380, lbl_804D7820, and lbl_804D7824.
Check whether GObj_GXFindPrioPosition remained inline/internal or was renamed in later gobjgxlink decomp work.
Compare later baselib gobjgxlink matches for consistent priority ordering semantics between GObj_SetupGXLink, GObj_SetupGXLinkMax, and GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted.
